destinations.evidence: The precompile fails without context, fails under 100 gas, and fails when context metadata lacks tx_hash.
The node executor populates async context tx_hash only when transaction metadata supplies one.
Recovered transaction conversion supplies signed transaction hashes for normal transaction execution paths.
The revm async context stores tx_hash metadata consumed by the precompile.

equivalence_argument: The TX_HASH behavior is owned by nodebuilder plus EVM/revm dependencies: signed transaction conversion carries tx_hash into TxEnv, the executor installs it into async context, and the precompile rejects absent context, absent metadata, and gas below 100.
notes: This maps to dependency repos because the precompile implementation lives outside the port workspace.
rationale: The fallback precompile returns an error without context, the context-aware implementation checks gas_limit and requires metadata().tx_hash, and system calls explicitly set tx_hash to None. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: The TX_HASH precompile must fail when called without execution context, without tx_hash metadata, or with less than 100 gas.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call tx_hash_precompile directly and assert it errors; call tx_hash_precompile_with_context with gas 99 and assert OutOfGas; call it with context metadata lacking tx_hash and assert it errors rather than returning zeros.
summary: TX_HASH fails without context, tx_hash metadata, or enough gas.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call tx_hash_precompile directly, with gas 99, and with context lacking tx_hash; assert errors, then call with tx_hash metadata and gas 100 and assert the hash bytes are returned.
title: TX_HASH fails without context or gas
